fab_section_md:
- King Aenys, obsessed with building the Red Keep, declared his descendants would rule from it for a thousand years (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:19)
- In 41 AC he announced the marriage of his daughter Rhaena to her brother Aegon, whom he named heir (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:19)
- Infamous for indecision, he neither abandoned the match nor burned the Starry Sept, but simply persisted (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:43)
- At the wedding feast he granted Aegon the title Prince of Dragonstone, which had belonged to Maegor (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:49)
- Denounced as "King Abomination," he fled to Dragonstone, fell sick with fear, and died (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:77)
identity_line: Aenys Targaryen — King Aenys, obsessed with building the Red Keep, declared his descendants would rule from it for a thousand years.

fab_section_md:
- Maegor, son of Visenya, returned from across the narrow sea on Balerion after Aenys's death and claimed the crown with his father's iron crown (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:83)
- He declared the throne goes to the strongest and beheaded Grand Maester Gawen who objected (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:89)
- He won the Trial of Seven against the Warrior's Sons, though grievously wounded (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:117)
- Recovering, he burned seven hundred Warrior's Sons in their sept, waged war on the Faith Militant, took Tyanna as his third wife, and slew Grand Maester Myros with Blackfyre (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:155)
identity_line: Maegor Targaryen — Maegor, son of Visenya, returned from across the narrow sea on Balerion after Aenys's death and claimed the crown with his father's iron crown.

fab_section_md:
- Queen Alyssa, widow of Aenys, sang his dirge at Dragonstone and took her younger children to her father's castle on Driftmark within hours of the funeral (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:91)
- She proclaimed her son Aegon the true king, but few heeded her (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:121)
- Persuaded by Visenya, she returned to court, did homage to Maegor, and was compelled to join his bedding (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:159)
identity_line: Alyssa Velaryon — Queen Alyssa, widow of Aenys, sang his dirge at Dragonstone and took her younger children to her father's castle on Driftmark within hours of the funeral.

fab_section_md:
- Tyanna, a pale raven-haired beauty from Pentos, arrived with Alys Harroway and six hundred sellswords (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:129)
- The natural daughter of a Pentoshi magister and a courtesan risen from tavern dancer, she was rumored a poisoner and sorceress; some called her Maegor's concubine, others Alys's paramour (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:131)
- Given charge of the dying king, she nursed him to recovery, became his third wife, and was called "the mistress of whispers"
identity_line: Tyanna of the Tower — Tyanna, a pale raven-haired beauty from Pentos, arrived with Alys Harroway and six hundred sellswords.

fab_section_md:
- Alys of House Harroway, daughter of Lucas, was Maegor's second wife; her father demanded the royal couple acknowledge her as their uncle's lawful wife (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:55)
- She returned from Pentos with Tyanna and presided over Maegor's bedding with Tyanna (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:159)
identity_line: Alys Harroway — Alys of House Harroway, daughter of Lucas, was Maegor's second wife; her father demanded the royal couple acknowledge her as their uncle's lawful wife.

fab_section_md:
- Ceryse of House Hightower, Maegor's first wife, insisted throughout that she remained his only lawful queen (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:163)
- Grand Maester Myros invoked her before Maegor killed him (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:153)
identity_line: Ceryse Hightower — Ceryse of House Hightower, Maegor's first wife, insisted throughout that she remained his only lawful queen.

fab_section_md:
- Wat the Hewer, a gigantic axeman, led nine thousand Poor Fellows caught crossing the Mander and cut to pieces at Stonebridge (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:125)
- Taken alive after slaying Lord Meadows and others, Maegor took off his limbs with his own axe but kept him alive to witness the royal wedding (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:153)
identity_line: Wat the Hewer, a gigantic axeman, led nine thousand Poor Fellows caught crossing the Mander and cut to pieces at Stonebridge.

fab_section_md: - Ser Damon Morrigen, called Damon the Devout, was Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons who led seven hundred knights down the Hill of Rhaenys and fought Maegor in the Trial of Seven, where he died (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:99)
identity_line: Damon Morrigen — Ser Damon Morrigen, called Damon the Devout, was Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons who led seven hundred knights down the Hill of Rhaenys and fought Maegor in the Trial of Seven, where he died.

fab_section_md:
- Dick Bean, a simple man-at-arms and no knight, was the first to stand beside Maegor at the Trial and the first to die (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:103)
- He declared he meant to die a king's man (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:105)
identity_line: Dick Bean, a simple man-at-arms and no knight, was the first to stand beside Maegor at the Trial and the first to die.

fab_section_md: - Ser Bernarr Brune, the squire who had slain Harren the Red and been knighted by Aenys, shamed the crowd into offering their swords for Maegor (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:109)
identity_line: Bernarr Brune — Ser Bernarr Brune, the squire who had slain Harren the Red and been knighted by Aenys, shamed the crowd into offering their swords for Maegor.

fab_section_md: - Grand Maester Myros alone dared speak openly against Maegor's marriage to Tyanna and was slain on the spot (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:155)
identity_line: Myros — Grand Maester Myros alone dared speak openly against Maegor's marriage to Tyanna and was slain on the spot.

fab_section_md: - Lord Lyman Lannister of Casterly Rock offered Aegon and Rhaena his protection and stood firm against Maegor's demand to return them in chains (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:125)
identity_line: Lyman Lannister — Lord Lyman Lannister of Casterly Rock offered Aegon and Rhaena his protection and stood firm against Maegor's demand to return them in chains.

fab_section_md: - Ser Morgan Hightower, younger brother of Lord Martyn, led two hundred Warrior's Sons to defend the Starry Sept and was among those suspected of the High Septon's death (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:213)
identity_line: Morgan Hightower — Ser Morgan Hightower, younger brother of Lord Martyn, led two hundred Warrior's Sons to defend the Starry Sept and was among those suspected of the High Septon's death.

fab_section_md: - Ser Joffrey Doggett, the Red Dog of the Hills, was chosen new grand captain of the Warrior's Sons and gathered two thousand men; Maegor later burned his family's seat and killed his kin (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:191)
identity_line: Joffrey Doggett — Ser Joffrey Doggett, the Red Dog of the Hills, was chosen new grand captain of the Warrior's Sons and gathered two thousand men; Maegor later burned his family's seat and killed his kin.

fab_section_md: - Ser Horys Hill, a hedge knight, led the western Poor Fellows and reached the Great Fork with thirteen thousand men; he escaped the battle but his following dwindled (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:147)
identity_line: Horys Hill — Ser Horys Hill, a hedge knight, led the western Poor Fellows and reached the Great Fork with thirteen thousand men; he escaped the battle but his following dwindled.

fab_section_md: - Lord Lucas Harroway of Harrenhal, father of Alys, refused the royal couple entry to his castle and was later made Maegor's Hand in 43 AC (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:181)
identity_line: Lucas Harroway — Lord Lucas Harroway of Harrenhal, father of Alys, refused the royal couple entry to his castle and was later made Maegor's Hand in 43 AC.

fab_section_md: - Lord Rupert Falwell, "the Fighting Fool," led the pious at the Great Fork and slew two Kingsguard before he was cut down by the Lord of Maidenpool (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:149)
identity_line: Rupert Falwell — Lord Rupert Falwell, "the Fighting Fool," led the pious at the Great Fork and slew two Kingsguard before he was cut down by the Lord of Maidenpool.

fab_section_md: - Lady Jocasta, wife of Lyman Lannister, first discerned that Princess Rhaena was with child (fab-sons-of-the-dragon-05-p02:125)
identity_line: Jocasta Lannister — Lady Jocasta, wife of Lyman Lannister, first discerned that Princess Rhaena was with child.
